mother.en: a woman who has a child

ex.en: Her mother is a teacher.

mother.en: look after someone in an overprotective way, like a mother

ex.en: Stop mothering me, I can take care of myself.
ex.ko: 나 좀 그만 과보호해, 나 스스로 챙길 수 있어.

motive.en: the reason that makes someone do something, especially one that isn't stated openly

ex.en: Police couldn't find a motive for the crime.
ex.ko: 경찰은 그 범죄의 동기를 찾지 못했다.

motive.en: causing or leading to action or movement

ex.en: Fear can be a powerful motive force.
ex.ko: 두려움은 강력한 원동력이 될 수 있다.

motor.en: a machine that changes power, such as electricity, into movement

ex.en: The washing machine's motor stopped working.
We motored down to the coast for the weekend.
ex.ko: 우리는 주말에 차를 몰고 해안가로 내려갔다.

motor.en: relating to the nerves that control muscle movement

ex.en: The accident damaged his motor nerves.
ex.ko: 그 사고로 그의 운동 신경이 손상되었다.

mouth.en: the opening in your face used for eating and speaking

ex.en: Open your mouth and say ah.

mouth.cue: 소리 내지 않고 입 모양으로만 말하다
mouth.en: form words with your lips without making any sound

ex.en: She mouthed "call me" across the room.
ex.ko: 그녀는 방 건너편에서 입 모양으로 '전화해'라고 말했다.

much.en: a large amount of something (used with uncountable nouns)

ex.en: I don't have much time today.

much.cue: 주어 자리에서 많은 양·정도를 가리킬 때
much.en: a large amount or part of something

ex.en: Much of the city was destroyed in the earthquake.
ex.ko: 그 도시의 많은 부분이 지진으로 파괴되었다.

much.cue: 불가산명사 앞에서 양의 많음을 나타내는 한정사
much.en: (used as a determiner before uncountable nouns) great in quantity

ex.en: We don't have much information about him.
ex.ko: 우리는 그에 대한 정보가 많지 않다.

mum.en: a British informal word for mother

ex.en: I'll ask my mum if I can come.
ex.ko: 내가 갈 수 있는지 엄마한테 물어볼게.

mum.cue: 놀라운 일을 비밀로 하고 말하지 않는
mum.en: not saying anything, especially about a secret (mainly in the phrase 'keep mum')

ex.en: Keep mum about the surprise party, okay?
ex.ko: 깜짝 파티에 대해서는 비밀로 해줘, 알았지?

ex.en: The station is near my house.
Come near so I can see you.
ex.ko: 내가 볼 수 있게 가까이 와.

near.en: come closer to something in distance or time

ex.en: The ship neared the harbor at sunset.

needle.en: a thin, sharp pointed tool used for sewing or giving injections

ex.en: The nurse used a needle to give the injection.

needle.en: a thin pointer on a dial or gauge that shows a measurement

ex.en: The needle on the fuel gauge was near empty.
ex.ko: 연료 계기판 바늘이 거의 바닥을 가리키고 있었다.

needle.en: annoy or provoke someone, especially by repeated teasing or criticism (informal)

ex.en: Stop needling your brother about his haircut.
ex.ko: 형 머리 스타일 가지고 그만 놀려.

negative.en: expressing disapproval, or having a bad or harmful effect

ex.en: She has a negative attitude about the plan.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 계획에 대해 부정적인 태도를 가지고 있다.

negative.en: (of a medical test) showing that a particular condition is not present

ex.en: His test result was negative.
The temperature dropped to negative five degrees.

negative.en: a piece of photographic film showing an image with light and dark reversed

ex.en: He kept the negatives from his old film camera.
ex.ko: 그는 오래된 필름 카메라의 원판들을 보관해 두었다.

net.en: material made of crossed strings or wires with spaces between them

ex.en: The fisherman threw his net into the water.
I found the information on the net.

net.en: remaining after all deductions, such as tax

ex.en: Her net income is $3,000 a month.

net.en: earn a specified amount as net profit

ex.en: The company netted $2 million last year.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 작년에 200만 달러의 순이익을 냈다.

new.cue: 만들어지거나 얻은 지 얼마 안 된
new.en: recently made, bought, or discovered; not old

ex.en: She bought a new phone.

new.en: very recently (used mainly in compounds like 'new-mown', 'new-fallen')

ex.en: The lawn was new-mown and smelled fresh.
ex.ko: 잔디를 갓 깎아서 신선한 냄새가 났다.

no.en: used to give a negative answer to a question

ex.en: "Do you like coffee?" "No, I don't."
There is no milk in the fridge.
The final count was ten yeses and five noes.

nonsense.en: ideas, words, or behavior that are silly or make no sense

ex.en: Stop talking nonsense and focus on the problem.

nonsense.en: having no clear or logical meaning

ex.en: He wrote a nonsense poem for the children.
ex.ko: 그는 아이들을 위해 뜻이 통하지 않는 말장난 시를 썼다.

northwest.en: the direction that is between north and west

ex.en: The wind is blowing from the northwest.

northwest.en: situated in or facing the northwest

ex.en: We drove through the northwest region.
The ship sailed northwest all night.

nose.en: the part of your face used for breathing and smelling

ex.en: She has a small nose.

nose.en: try to find out about someone else's private business (informal, 'nose into/around')

ex.en: She's always nosing into other people's business.
I have to go now.
Let's just focus on the here and now.

number.en: a word or symbol that represents an amount or quantity

ex.en: Choose a number between one and ten.

number.en: a series of digits used to identify something, such as a phone or a seat

ex.en: Can I have your phone number?

number.en: give a number to something, especially to show its order

ex.en: Number each page in the top corner.

nutrient.en: a substance that living things need to grow and stay healthy

ex.en: Vegetables are rich in essential nutrients.
Plants absorb nutrient minerals from the soil.
ex.ko: 식물은 토양에서 영양이 되는 무기질을 흡수한다.

officer.en: a member of the police force

ex.en: A police officer stopped the car.

officer.en: a person who holds a position of command in the armed forces

ex.en: The officer gave orders to the soldiers.

officer.en: command or lead as an officer (formal, rare)

ex.en: The unit was officered by experienced veterans.
ex.ko: 그 부대는 노련한 참전 용사들이 지휘했다.

offshore.en: in the sea, not far from the coast

ex.en: They found oil in an offshore drilling site.
ex.ko: 그들은 앞바다의 시추 현장에서 석유를 발견했다.

offshore.cue: 세금 등을 피해 다른 나라에 두는
offshore.en: based in another country, especially to avoid tax or regulations

ex.en: The company moved its profits to an offshore account.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 수익을 역외 계좌로 옮겼다.

offshore.en: away from the coast, out at sea

ex.en: The boat drifted offshore during the storm.
ex.ko: 그 배는 폭풍 속에서 먼바다 쪽으로 떠내려갔다.

oil.en: a thick liquid used in cooking, made from plants or animals

ex.en: She fried the vegetables in oil.

oil.en: a thick, dark liquid found underground that is used as fuel

ex.en: The country's economy depends on oil exports.
ex.ko: 그 나라의 경제는 석유 수출에 의존한다.

oil.en: put oil on something, such as a machine, to make it work smoothly

ex.en: He oiled the bicycle chain before the ride.
ex.ko: 그는 타기 전에 자전거 체인에 기름칠을 했다.

on.en: positioned on top of or touching a surface

ex.en: The book is on the table.

on.en: used before a day or date to say when something happens

ex.en: We have a meeting on Monday.
Please turn the light on.

on.en: (of an event) planned or scheduled to happen

ex.en: Is the meeting still on for tomorrow?
I only need one ticket.

one.cue: 정해지지 않은 특정한 날·사람을 가리킬 때
one.en: a certain, but not specified, day or person

ex.en: One day, everything will make sense.
ex.ko: 언젠가는 모든 게 이해가 될 것이다.

open.en: move something so it is no longer closed

ex.en: Can you open the window?
The door was open when I arrived.
ex.ko: 내가 도착했을 때 문이 열려 있었다.

ex.en: The store is open until nine tonight.

open.en: a state in which information is no longer hidden and has become public

ex.en: Finally, the truth was out in the open.

opening.en: a space or hole that something can pass through

ex.en: Light came through a small opening in the wall.
ex.ko: 벽에 난 작은 구멍으로 빛이 들어왔다.

opening.en: the start or first event of something, such as an exhibition or store

ex.en: We attended the opening of the new museum.

opening.en: a job or position that is available

ex.en: There is an opening for a marketing manager.

opening.en: coming first, at the beginning of something

ex.en: The opening scene of the movie was unforgettable.
ex.ko: 그 영화의 첫 장면은 잊을 수 없었다.

orderly.en: well organized, or behaving in a calm and controlled way

ex.en: The students left the building in an orderly manner.

orderly.cue: 병원에서 환자 이동 등을 돕는 사람
orderly.en: a hospital worker who does general, non-medical duties

ex.en: An orderly wheeled the patient to the operating room.

ordinary.en: not special or different in any way; normal

ex.en: It was just an ordinary day at work.

ordinary.cue: 평소와 다르지 않은 상태를 가리킬 때 (out of the ordinary)
ordinary.en: the usual or expected state of things

ex.en: Nothing out of the ordinary happened today.
ex.ko: 오늘은 평소와 다른 특별한 일이 없었다.

output.cue: 일정 기간 동안 만들어 낸 양
output.en: the amount of something produced by a person, machine, or country

ex.en: The factory increased its output by 20 percent.

output.en: the signal or power that comes out of an electronic device or system

ex.en: Check the audio output settings on your computer.
ex.ko: 컴퓨터의 오디오 출력 설정을 확인해 보세요.

output.en: produce or create a specified amount of something

ex.en: The printer outputs ten pages a minute.
ex.ko: 그 프린터는 분당 열 장을 출력한다.

outrage.en: a strong feeling of anger and shock caused by something unfair or wrong

ex.en: The decision caused public outrage.

outrage.en: a shocking, cruel, or unacceptable act

ex.en: The attack on civilians was described as an outrage.
ex.ko: 민간인에 대한 그 공격은 만행이라고 표현되었다.

outrage.en: make someone feel extreme anger and shock

ex.en: The unfair verdict outraged the public.

outward.en: relating to how someone or something appears, rather than what is actually true inside

ex.en: Despite his outward calm, he was very nervous.
ex.ko: 겉으로는 침착해 보였지만 그는 매우 긴장하고 있었다.

outward.en: going away from a place, especially at the start of a journey

ex.en: The outward flight was delayed by two hours.

outward.en: toward the outside or away from the center

ex.en: The door swings outward.
The table has an oval shape.

oval.en: a rounded shape like an egg, longer than it is wide

ex.en: Draw an oval on the paper.
The overall quality is good.

overall.cue: 작업할 때 겉에 입는 헐렁한 옷
overall.en: loose protective clothing worn over other clothes for dirty work

ex.en: He wore overalls while painting the fence.
ex.ko: 그는 울타리를 칠할 때 작업복을 입었다.

overlap.en: a representation of common ground between theories or phenomena

ex.en: There is some overlap between the two topics.
ex.ko: 그 두 주제 사이에는 약간의 겹침이 있어요.

overlap.en: cover part of the same area or period of time as something else

ex.en: Our schedules overlap on Tuesdays.
The rebels tried to overthrow the government.

overthrow.en: the act of removing a ruler or government from power by force

ex.en: The coup led to the overthrow of the dictator.

pacific.en: relating to or bordering the Pacific Ocean

ex.en: Many islands are located in the Pacific region.
ex.ko: 많은 섬들이 태평양 지역에 위치해 있다.

ex.en: He is known for his pacific nature.

pacific.en: the largest ocean in the world

ex.en: The Pacific is the largest ocean in the world.

package.en: a box or container in which something is wrapped or sent

ex.en: A package arrived for you this morning.
ex.ko: 오늘 아침에 당신 앞으로 소포가 도착했어요.

package.en: a set of things offered or sold together as a single unit

ex.en: The hotel offers a package that includes breakfast and a tour.
ex.ko: 그 호텔은 조식과 투어가 포함된 패키지를 제공한다.

package.en: put something into a box or wrapping

ex.en: The products are packaged before shipping.

pad.en: a number of sheets of paper fastened together along one edge

ex.en: He wrote notes on a pad.

pad.en: fill or cover something with soft material

ex.en: The jacket is padded for warmth.
ex.ko: 그 재킷은 보온을 위해 속을 채워 두툼하게 만들었다.

page.en: one side of a sheet of paper in a book

ex.en: Please turn to page ten.

page.en: call someone's name over a public address system or contact them by pager

ex.en: The nurse paged Dr. Kim to the emergency room.
ex.ko: 간호사는 무전으로 김 박사를 응급실로 호출했다.

pain.en: the physical feeling of being hurt or sick

ex.en: She felt a sharp pain in her back.

pain.en: someone or something that is very annoying (informal)

ex.en: This broken printer is such a pain.

pain.en: cause someone physical or emotional suffering (formal)

ex.en: It pains me to see him suffer like this.
ex.ko: 그가 이렇게 고통받는 걸 보니 마음이 아프다.

pair.en: two things of the same kind that go together

ex.en: I bought a new pair of shoes.
ex.ko: 나는 새 신발 한 켤레를 샀다.

pair.en: put two things or people together to form a pair

ex.en: The app pairs users with similar interests.
ex.ko: 그 앱은 비슷한 관심사를 가진 사용자들을 짝지어준다.

pale.en: having skin that is lighter in color than usual, often because of illness or fear

ex.en: She looked pale after hearing the bad news.
ex.ko: 그녀는 나쁜 소식을 듣고 창백해 보였다.

pale.en: (of a color) light rather than dark or bright

ex.en: The walls were painted a pale blue.

pale.en: become pale, especially from fear or shock

ex.en: He paled at the sight of the accident.

pale.cue: 울타리를 이루는 나무 말뚝 (주로 'beyond the pale' 관용구로)
pale.en: a wooden post used to make a fence, mainly used today in the phrase 'beyond the pale' (unacceptable)

ex.en: His comments were beyond the pale.

palm.en: the inner, flat surface of your hand

ex.en: She held the coin in the palm of her hand.

palm.en: a tall tropical tree with large leaves at the top and no branches

ex.en: Palm trees lined the beach.

palm.en: hold or hide something in your hand, often secretly

ex.en: The magician palmed the coin skillfully.
ex.ko: 그 마술사는 능숙하게 동전을 손에 감췄다.

pan.en: a wide metal container used for cooking food

ex.en: Heat some oil in the pan.
Critics panned the movie for its weak plot.
ex.ko: 평론가들은 그 영화의 부실한 줄거리를 혹평했다.

panel.en: sheet that forms a distinct (usually flat and rectangular) section or component of something

ex.en: A panel of experts discussed the topic.
ex.ko: 전문가 패널이 그 주제에 대해 논의했어요.

panel.en: decorate a surface, such as a wall, with panels

ex.en: The walls were paneled with dark wood.
ex.ko: 그 벽들은 짙은 색 나무 패널로 장식되었다.

parade.en: a ceremonial procession including people marching

ex.en: We watched the parade downtown.

parade.en: walk somewhere in a way meant to attract attention

ex.en: He paraded around in his new suit.
ex.ko: 그는 새 정장을 입고 뽐내며 돌아다녔다.

parcel.en: something wrapped in paper or a box, especially to be sent by mail

ex.en: The postman delivered a parcel to our house.

parcel.en: divide something into parts and share it out (often 'parcel out')

ex.en: The land was parceled out among the heirs.
ex.ko: 그 땅은 상속인들 사이에 나누어 배분되었다.

part.en: leave someone or stop being together

ex.en: The two friends parted at the station.

part.en: a piece or section of something that combines with other pieces to make a whole

ex.en: This is the best part of the movie.
ex.ko: 이게 그 영화에서 가장 좋은 부분이야.

part.en: a piece that is used to make a machine or object

ex.en: We need to order some car parts.
ex.ko: 우리는 자동차 부품을 좀 주문해야 한다.

part.en: the character that an actor plays in a film or play

ex.en: She played the part of the queen.
The film is part documentary, part fiction.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 부분적으로 다큐멘터리이고 부분적으로 허구다.

particular.en: unique or specific to a person or thing or category

ex.en: She is particular about her food.

particular.en: a detailed piece of information about someone or something

ex.en: Please provide your particulars for the application.
ex.ko: 신청을 위해 상세 정보를 제공해 주세요.

partner.en: a person who works with you or does an activity together with you

ex.en: Choose a partner for the dance.

partner.en: the person someone is married to or in a romantic relationship with

ex.en: She introduced me to her partner.

partner.cue: 두 대상을 짝지어 주거나 협력 관계가 되다
partner.en: join with someone else, or make people or organizations work together

ex.en: She partnered with a local charity for the event.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 행사를 위해 지역 자선단체와 협력했다.

pattern.en: a repeated design of shapes, lines, or colors on something

ex.en: She chose a fabric with a floral pattern.

pattern.en: a regular way in which something happens or is done

ex.en: Researchers noticed a pattern in the data.

pattern.en: make or design something based on a model or example

ex.en: The new law was patterned after similar legislation in Europe.
ex.ko: 그 새 법은 유럽의 비슷한 법을 본떠 만들어졌다.

pen.en: a tool used for writing with ink

ex.en: Can I borrow your pen?

pen.en: write something, such as a letter or book (formal)

ex.en: She penned a heartfelt letter to her mother.
ex.ko: 그녀는 어머니께 진심 어린 편지를 썼다.

ex.en: Many people were waiting at the station.

people.en: fill a place with people, or cause people to live there (formal, literary)

ex.en: The island was peopled by early settlers.

pepper.en: a spicy powder used to flavor food, made from dried berries

ex.en: Add some salt and pepper to the soup.

pepper.en: a hollow vegetable, often red, green, or yellow, that can be mild or spicy

ex.en: She chopped a red pepper for the salad.

pepper.en: direct a rapid series of things, such as questions, at someone

ex.en: Reporters peppered the minister with questions.

pet.en: an animal that people keep at home for companionship

ex.en: Do you have any pets?

pet.en: touch or stroke an animal or person gently and affectionately

ex.en: She gently petted the cat.

phrase.en: a group of words that together express an idea but is not a full sentence

ex.en: "In the morning" is a useful phrase to know.

phrase.en: express something in a particular way using words

ex.en: He phrased his criticism very carefully.
ex.ko: 그는 자신의 비판을 매우 신중하게 표현했다.

picnic.en: an outdoor meal or trip where people eat food outside

ex.en: We went on a picnic by the lake.

picnic.en: have a meal outdoors, especially on a trip

ex.en: We picnicked by the river.

piece.en: a part of something that has been separated from a whole

ex.en: Would you like a piece of cake?

piece.en: a single work of music, art, or writing

ex.en: The pianist played a beautiful piece.

piece.en: put together separate pieces of information to understand the whole story

ex.en: Detectives pieced together the events of that night.

pilot.en: a person who is trained and licensed to fly an aircraft

ex.en: The pilot announced a delay before takeoff.

pilot.en: a small trial version of something done before starting it fully

ex.en: They launched a pilot program in three schools.
ex.ko: 그들은 세 개 학교에서 시범 프로그램을 시작했다.

ex.en: She piloted the small plane across the country.
ex.ko: 그녀는 소형 비행기를 조종해 전국을 횡단했다.

pipe.en: a long tube made of metal or plastic used to carry water, oil, or gas

ex.en: Water flows through pipes under the street.
ex.ko: 물은 거리 아래의 파이프를 통해 흐른다.

pipe.en: a small object with a tube and bowl used for smoking tobacco

ex.en: His grandfather always smoked a pipe after dinner.
ex.ko: 그의 할아버지는 항상 저녁 식사 후 파이프 담배를 피웠다.

pipe.en: carry a liquid or gas through pipes

ex.en: Water is piped directly to each house.
ex.ko: 물은 각 집에 파이프로 직접 공급된다.

pit.en: a large hole in the ground

ex.en: The workers dug a deep pit to bury the pipes.
ex.ko: 인부들은 파이프를 묻기 위해 깊은 구덩이를 팠다.

pit.en: the hard seed found in the middle of some fruits, such as peaches or cherries

ex.en: Be careful not to bite the pit in the cherry.
ex.ko: 체리 안에 있는 씨를 깨물지 않게 조심해.

pit.en: put two people or things into a competition against each other (often 'pit against')

ex.en: The tournament pits the two best teams against each other.
ex.ko: 그 대회는 최고의 두 팀을 맞붙게 한다.

pity.en: a feeling of sadness for someone else's suffering

ex.en: She felt pity for the homeless man.

pity.en: an unfortunate situation that causes disappointment

ex.en: It's a pity you can't come to the party.
I pity anyone who has to work with him.
ex.ko: 그와 함께 일해야 하는 사람이 누구든 안됐다.

plane.en: a vehicle with wings that flies through the air

ex.en: We took a plane to New York.

plane.en: make wood smooth by cutting off thin layers with a plane tool

ex.en: He planed the wood until it was smooth.

plane.en: having a flat, even surface with no part higher or lower than another

ex.en: The tabletop must be perfectly plane.
ex.ko: 그 탁자 상판은 완전히 평평해야 한다.

plenty.en: a large amount that is more than enough

ex.en: There's plenty of food for everyone.

plenty.cue: (구어) 강조를 위해 형용사 앞에서 충분히, 아주
plenty.en: quite; more than enough (informal, before an adjective)

ex.en: This box is plenty big enough for all your books.
ex.ko: 이 상자는 네 책을 다 담기에 충분히 크다.

plum.en: a small, round, juicy fruit with smooth purple or red skin

ex.en: She picked ripe plums from the tree.
ex.ko: 그녀는 나무에서 잘 익은 자두를 땄다.

plum.en: (of a job or position) highly desirable

ex.en: He landed a plum job at a top law firm.
ex.ko: 그는 최고 로펌에서 탐나는 자리를 얻었다.

ex.en: He was plum tired out after the race.

pocket.en: a small pouch sewn into clothing for carrying things

ex.en: He put his keys in his pocket.

pocket.cue: 돈 등을 부당하게 자기 몫으로 챙기다
pocket.en: put something in your pocket, or take money dishonestly for yourself

ex.en: He pocketed the extra change without saying anything.
ex.ko: 그는 아무 말 없이 남은 잔돈을 챙겼다.

pole.en: a long, thin, straight piece of wood, metal, or plastic

ex.en: They used a long pole to reach the fruit on the tree.
ex.ko: 그들은 나무의 과일에 닿기 위해 긴 장대를 사용했다.

pole.en: either end of the earth's axis, or either end of a magnet

ex.en: Polar bears live near the North Pole.

pole.en: push a boat along using a long pole

ex.en: They poled the boat along the canal.
ex.ko: 그들은 장대로 배를 밀며 운하를 따라갔다.

police.en: the official organization responsible for keeping order and enforcing the law

ex.en: The police arrived quickly after the accident.

police.en: keep an area safe and orderly by patrolling or supervising it

ex.en: Volunteers police the neighborhood at night.

polish.en: rub something to make it smooth and shiny

ex.en: He polished his shoes before the interview.
ex.ko: 그는 면접 전에 구두를 광이 나게 닦았다.

polish.en: a smooth, shiny surface, or a substance used to produce it

ex.en: The table has a beautiful polish.
ex.ko: 그 탁자는 아름다운 광택을 지니고 있다.

polish.en: improve something by making small changes and corrections

ex.en: She polished her essay before submitting it.

polish.en: relating to Poland, its people, or its language (capitalized: Polish)

ex.en: She speaks Polish fluently.

pool.en: a large container filled with water for swimming

ex.en: The kids are swimming in the pool.

pool.en: a small area of still water

ex.en: There was a pool of water after the rain.

pool.en: put money or resources together with others for a shared purpose

ex.en: They pooled their money to buy a gift.

position.en: the place where someone or something is

ex.en: Mark the position of the treasure on the map.

position.en: a job or role within an organization

ex.en: She applied for a position at the bank.

position.en: the way someone's body is arranged

ex.en: He changed his sleeping position.

position.en: an opinion or attitude about something

ex.en: What is your position on this issue?
ex.ko: 이 문제에 대한 당신의 입장은 무엇인가요?

position.en: put someone or something in a particular place

ex.en: Position the camera facing the door.

possible.en: able to happen, exist, or be done

ex.en: Is it possible to finish this by tomorrow?

possible.en: someone who might be suitable for a team or position

ex.en: The coach is scouting several possibles for the squad.
ex.ko: 그 감독은 팀에 넣을 만한 후보 몇 명을 살펴보고 있다.

pot.en: a round metal or ceramic container used for cooking food

ex.en: She put the pot of soup on the stove.

pot.en: a container in which a plant is grown

ex.en: He planted the flower in a small pot.
She potted the young tomato plants.
ex.ko: 그녀는 어린 토마토 모종을 화분에 심었다.

power.en: the ability to do something or make things happen

ex.en: Knowledge is power.

power.en: control or authority over people or events

ex.en: The new president has a lot of power.
ex.ko: 새 대통령은 많은 권력을 가지고 있다.

power.en: energy, especially electricity, used to make things work

ex.en: The storm cut off the power to our house.

power.en: supply the energy needed to make something work

ex.en: Solar panels power the entire house.
ex.ko: 태양광 패널이 집 전체에 동력을 공급한다.

preserve.en: keep something in its original state or protect it from damage or destruction

ex.en: We must preserve the forest for future generations.
ex.ko: 우리는 미래 세대를 위해 숲을 보존해야 한다.

preserve.en: fruit that has been cooked with sugar so it can be kept for a long time

ex.en: She spread strawberry preserves on her toast.

pressure.en: the force produced by pressing on something

ex.en: Check the tire pressure before you drive.

pressure.en: a feeling of stress caused by difficulties or demands

ex.en: She's under a lot of pressure at work.
ex.ko: 그녀는 직장에서 큰 압박을 받고 있다.

pressure.en: try to force someone to do something

ex.en: His parents pressured him to study medicine.
ex.ko: 그의 부모님은 그에게 의학을 공부하라고 압박했다.

pride.en: a feeling of pleasure or satisfaction from your own achievements, qualities, or possessions

ex.en: She takes great pride in her work.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 일에 큰 자부심을 느낀다.

pride.en: feel proud of a particular quality or skill you have (pride oneself on)

ex.en: She prides herself on her attention to detail.

prime.en: main, most important, or of the best quality

ex.en: This is a prime example of good design.

prime.en: (mathematics) a number that can only be divided evenly by 1 and itself

ex.en: Seven is a prime number.

prime.en: the period when someone is at their strongest, most successful, or most attractive

ex.en: The athlete is now in the prime of his career.
ex.ko: 그 선수는 지금 커리어의 전성기를 맞고 있다.

prime.en: cover a surface with a first layer of paint before the main coat

ex.en: Prime the wall before painting it.

private.en: belonging to or concerning one particular person, not for public use

ex.en: This is a private conversation.

private.en: a soldier of the lowest rank in the army

ex.en: He joined the army as a private.

pro.en: a person who does something as their profession, especially in sports (informal)

ex.en: She turned pro at the age of eighteen.

pro.en: in favor of a plan, action, or proposal

ex.en: Are you pro or against the new policy?
ex.ko: 너는 그 새 정책에 찬성이야, 반대야?

provision.en: a clause in a law, contract, or agreement that states a condition

ex.en: The contract includes a provision for early termination.
ex.ko: 그 계약에는 조기 종료에 관한 조항이 있다.

provision.en: the act of supplying or providing something needed

ex.en: The charity focuses on the provision of clean water.
ex.ko: 그 자선단체는 깨끗한 물 공급에 주력한다.

provision.en: a supply of food and other necessary items

ex.en: They packed enough provisions for a week.

provision.en: supply someone with the things they need (formal)

ex.en: The ship was provisioned for a six-month voyage.
ex.ko: 그 배는 6개월간의 항해를 위한 물자가 실렸다.

pull.en: use force to move something toward yourself

ex.en: Pull the door to open it.

pull.cue: (구어) 남에게 영향을 줄 수 있는 힘, 연줄
pull.en: special influence over people or events (informal)

ex.en: He has a lot of pull in the industry.

purse.en: a small bag used for carrying money and personal items, especially by women

ex.en: She kept her keys in her purse.

purse.en: press your lips together tightly, often to show disapproval

ex.en: She pursed her lips in disapproval.

quake.en: a sudden shaking of the ground, short for earthquake

ex.en: The quake shook buildings across the city.
ex.ko: 그 지진은 도시 전역의 건물을 흔들었다.

ex.en: He quaked with fear before the interview.

quick.en: done or happening in a short time

ex.en: Let's take a quick break.
Come quick, the bus is leaving!
Please be quiet in the library.

quiet.en: a state of silence or calm

ex.en: I enjoy the quiet of the early morning.

quiet.en: become calm and quiet, or make someone calm and quiet

ex.en: The teacher asked the students to quiet down.
Please talk quiet, the baby is sleeping.

radio.en: a device that receives broadcast sound programs

ex.en: He listens to the radio while driving.

radio.en: send a message to someone using radio waves

ex.en: The pilot radioed the control tower for permission to land.
ex.ko: 조종사는 관제탑에 착륙 허가를 요청하는 무전을 보냈다.

ex.en: Radio isotopes are used in some medical treatments.
ex.ko: 방사성 동위원소는 일부 의료 치료에 사용된다.

rage.en: a strong feeling of extreme anger

ex.en: He shouted in a fit of rage.

rage.en: feel or express violent anger, or (of a storm etc.) continue with great force

ex.en: He raged at the unfair decision.

rail.en: a bar fixed along or around something for people to hold onto

ex.en: Hold the rail as you go down the stairs.

rail.en: a metal bar that forms the track for a train

ex.en: The train runs smoothly along the rails.

rail.en: complain about something in an angry, forceful way (formal, 'rail against')

ex.en: Critics railed against the new policy.

rank.en: a position or level in an organization, such as the army

ex.en: He rose to the rank of captain.

rank.en: give something a position on a scale compared with others

ex.en: The team ranks first in the league.
The rank smell of the swamp filled the air.
ex.ko: 늪의 역겨운 냄새가 공기를 가득 채웠다.

ready.en: prepared and able to do something

ex.en: Are you ready to go?

ready.cue: 무기 등을 즉시 쓸 수 있게 준비된 상태 (at the ready)
ready.en: a state of being fully prepared for immediate action

ex.en: The soldiers stood with weapons at the ready.
ex.ko: 병사들은 무기를 만반의 준비 상태로 들고 서 있었다.

ready.en: make someone or something prepared for something

ex.en: She readied herself for the interview.

reason.en: the cause or explanation for something

ex.en: What is the reason for the delay?

reason.en: think about something in a logical way to reach a conclusion

ex.en: He reasoned that the delay was due to traffic.
ex.ko: 그는 지연이 교통 체증 때문이라고 추론했다.

reform.en: a change for the better as a result of correcting abuses

ex.en: The government proposed education reform.

reform.en: make changes to something, especially a system or organization, in order to improve it

ex.en: The government aims to reform the tax system.

refrain.en: the part of a song where a soloist is joined by a group of singers

ex.en: Please refrain from talking during the movie.
ex.ko: 영화 상영 중 대화를 삼가 주세요.

refrain.cue: 어떤 행동을 하지 않도록 스스로 자제하다

ex.en: Please refrain from smoking in this area.

refund.en: an amount of money given back to you, for example because you returned a product

ex.en: She asked for a refund on the broken item.
ex.ko: 그녀는 고장난 물건에 대해 환불을 요청했다.

refund.en: give money back to someone, for example because they returned a product

ex.en: The store refunded my money for the broken item.
ex.ko: 그 가게는 고장난 물건에 대해 내게 환불해 주었다.

regular.en: happening often, at fixed times, or in a fixed pattern

ex.en: Regular exercise keeps you healthy.

regular.en: a customer who goes to a particular place often

ex.en: He's a regular at that coffee shop.

regulation.en: an official rule that controls how something is done

ex.en: The factory must follow safety regulations.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 안전 규정을 따라야 한다.

regulation.en: matching the official rules or standard, especially about size

ex.en: Players must wear a regulation uniform.
ex.ko: 선수들은 규정에 맞는 유니폼을 입어야 한다.

relay.en: the act of passing something along from one person or group to another

ex.en: Our team won the relay race.

relay.en: pass a message or information from one person to another

ex.en: She relayed the message to her boss.

remedy.en: something that cures an illness or solves a problem

ex.en: Honey and tea is a common remedy for a sore throat.
ex.ko: 꿀차는 목 아플 때 흔히 쓰는 치료법이다.

remedy.en: correct or improve a bad situation

ex.en: The company took steps to remedy the situation.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 상황을 바로잡기 위한 조치를 취했다.

ex.en: Could you repeat that, please?

repeat.cue: 이전에 방송된 것을 다시 방송하는 것 (재방송)
repeat.en: a television or radio program that is broadcast again

ex.en: This is a repeat of an episode from last season.

representative.en: a person chosen to speak or act on behalf of others

ex.en: A representative from the company attended the meeting.

representative.en: typical of a particular group, class, or style

ex.en: This painting is representative of his early style.
ex.ko: 이 그림은 그의 초기 화풍을 대표적으로 보여준다.

reprint.en: a publication (such as a book) that is reprinted without changes or editing and offered again for sale

ex.en: This book is a reprint of the 1980 edition.

reprint.en: print a book or document again, usually without changes

ex.en: The publisher plans to reprint the bestselling novel.
ex.ko: 그 출판사는 베스트셀러 소설을 재판 찍을 계획이다.

resolve.en: find a solution to a problem or disagreement

ex.en: They managed to resolve the conflict peacefully.

resolve.en: make a firm decision to do something

ex.en: She resolved to exercise every morning.

resolve.en: firm determination to do something despite difficulty

ex.en: Nothing could shake her resolve.
ex.ko: 그 무엇도 그녀의 결의를 흔들지 못했다.

ex.en: The meeting will resume after lunch.

resume.en: a short written summary of a person's education and work experience

ex.en: She sent her resume to several companies.

retail.en: the selling of goods to customers in stores, not in large quantities to businesses

ex.en: He works in retail at a clothing store.

retail.en: sell goods directly to customers, often for a stated price

ex.en: The shoes retail for about $80.

revenge.en: action taken to hurt someone in return for a wrong they did to you

ex.en: She wanted revenge for what he had done to her.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그가 자신에게 한 짓에 대해 복수하고 싶었다.

revenge.en: hurt or punish someone in return for what they did to you

ex.en: He revenged himself on his old rival.

reverence.en: a feeling of deep respect or awe for someone or something

ex.en: He spoke of his teacher with great reverence.
ex.ko: 그는 자신의 스승에 대해 깊은 존경심을 담아 말했다.

reverence.en: regard someone or something with deep respect (formal)

ex.en: The villagers reverenced the old temple.

rim.en: the outer edge of something round, such as a cup, wheel, or glasses

ex.en: She wiped the rim of the glass.

rim.en: form or go around the edge of something

ex.en: Trees rimmed the edge of the lake.

romantic.en: relating to or expressing feelings of love

ex.en: They had a romantic dinner by candlelight.
ex.ko: 그들은 촛불 아래에서 낭만적인 저녁 식사를 했다.

romantic.en: not sensible or realistic about practical matters

ex.en: His romantic view of farming ignored the hard work involved.
ex.ko: 농사에 대한 그의 이상주의적인 시각은 힘든 노동을 간과했다.

romantic.en: a person who has romantic or idealistic feelings about life

ex.en: He's a hopeless romantic who believes in true love.
ex.ko: 그는 진정한 사랑을 믿는 구제불능 로맨티스트다.

roof.en: the covering on top of a building or vehicle

ex.en: Snow covered the roof of the house.

roof.en: an upper limit on an amount, such as prices or spending

ex.en: The government set a roof on rent increases.

roof.en: cover a building with a roof

ex.en: The barn was roofed with tin sheets.

rose.en: a flower with a sweet smell that grows on a bush with thorns

ex.en: He gave her a bouquet of red roses.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀에게 빨간 장미 꽃다발을 주었다.

ex.en: She wore a rose-colored dress to the wedding.
ex.ko: 그녀는 결혼식에 장밋빛 드레스를 입고 갔다.

rough.en: having an uneven or bumpy surface, not smooth

ex.en: The rock had a rough surface.
ex.ko: 그 바위는 거친 표면을 가지고 있었다.

rough.en: not exact or precise, only approximate

ex.en: Can you give me a rough estimate of the cost?

rough.en: make or draw something in a quick, basic form

ex.en: Let's rough out a plan before the meeting.
ex.ko: 회의 전에 대충 계획을 잡아 보자.

rough.en: in a rough or violent way (informal, nonstandard variant of 'roughly')

ex.en: Don't play so rough with your little brother.

routine.en: the usual set of things you do regularly, especially every day

ex.en: Exercise is part of her daily routine.

routine.en: happening as a regular, normal part of a process, not for a special reason

ex.en: It was just a routine checkup at the doctor's.

salt.en: a white substance used to flavor or preserve food

ex.en: Add a pinch of salt to the soup.

salt.en: add salt to food, or spread salt on a surface such as an icy road

ex.en: Salt the water before boiling the pasta.
ex.ko: 파스타를 삶기 전에 물에 소금을 넣어라.

salt.en: (of speech) sharp, bitter, or painful (literary, rare)

ex.en: His salt remarks stung everyone in the room.
ex.ko: 그의 신랄한 말은 방 안의 모든 사람에게 상처를 주었다.

saving.en: money that you keep instead of spending, or the act of not spending money

ex.en: She puts some of her salary into savings every month.

saving.en: an amount of money or time that is not used or wasted

ex.en: Buying in bulk gives a saving of about 15%.
ex.ko: 대량으로 사면 약 15%를 절약할 수 있다.

saving.cue: 결점을 상쇄해 주는, 구제해 주는 (saving grace)
saving.en: making up for a fault or weakness in someone or something (mainly 'saving grace')

ex.en: His sense of humor was his saving grace.
ex.ko: 그의 유머 감각이 그의 유일한 장점이었다.

scheme.en: an organized plan or system for doing something

ex.en: The government introduced a new pension scheme.
He was involved in a scheme to defraud investors.

scheme.en: make secret plans, often to do something dishonest

ex.en: They schemed to take over the company.

school.en: a place where children go to learn

ex.en: He walks to school every morning.

school.en: train or teach someone in a particular skill

ex.en: He was schooled in the art of negotiation.

sculpture.en: a work of art made by carving or shaping stone, wood, or another material

ex.en: The museum displays ancient Greek sculptures.
ex.ko: 그 박물관은 고대 그리스 조각품을 전시한다.

sculpture.en: create a work of art by carving or shaping material such as stone or wood

ex.en: The artist sculptured a figure from marble.

seat.en: a place where you can sit

ex.en: Please take a seat.

seat.cue: 사람을 자리에 앉히다, 좌석을 몇 개 수용하다
seat.en: show someone to a seat, or have a certain number of seats

ex.en: The stadium seats over 50,000 people.

self.en: a person's own individual identity or nature

ex.en: She wants to find her true self.

self.cue: (문어체, 드묾) 전체가 한 가지 색이나 재질로 된
self.en: made entirely of one uniform color or material (literary, rare)

ex.en: She wore a self-colored blouse and skirt.
ex.ko: 그녀는 한 가지 색으로만 된 블라우스와 치마를 입었다.

sequence.en: the order in which things happen or are arranged

ex.en: Follow the correct sequence of steps.

sequence.en: a series of related shots or events in a film

ex.en: The chase sequence was thrilling to watch.
ex.ko: 그 추격 장면은 손에 땀을 쥐게 했다.

sequence.cue: 유전자 등의 서열을 분석하다, 순서대로 배열하다
sequence.en: arrange things in a particular order, or determine the order of units in something like DNA

ex.en: Scientists sequenced the entire human genome.
ex.ko: 과학자들은 인간 게놈 전체의 서열을 분석했다.

shame.en: a painful feeling caused by knowing you have done something wrong or foolish

ex.en: He felt shame after lying to his friend.
ex.ko: 그는 친구에게 거짓말한 것에 대해 부끄러움을 느꼈다.

ex.en: It's a shame you can't come to the party.

shame.en: make someone feel ashamed, often so they do something

ex.en: He was shamed into apologizing.
ex.ko: 그는 망신을 당해 사과할 수밖에 없었다.

sheer.en: complete and total, used to emphasize something

ex.en: It was sheer luck that we found the place.
ex.ko: 우리가 그곳을 찾은 건 순전히 운이었다.

sheer.en: rising or falling very steeply, almost straight up or down

ex.en: The sheer cliff dropped straight into the sea.

sheer.en: (of fabric) so thin that light passes through it

ex.en: She wore a sheer white blouse.
ex.ko: 그녀는 속이 비치는 흰색 블라우스를 입었다.

sheer.en: turn suddenly and sharply, especially to avoid something

ex.en: The ship sheered off to avoid the rocks.
ex.ko: 그 배는 바위를 피하려고 급히 방향을 틀었다.

ex.en: The soldier carried a shield.

shield.en: protect someone or something from danger or harm

ex.en: She shielded her eyes from the sun.
ex.ko: 그녀는 손으로 눈을 가려 햇빛을 막았다.

shirt.en: a piece of clothing worn on the upper body, often with a collar and buttons

ex.en: He wore a blue shirt to the interview.
ex.ko: 그는 면접에 파란 셔츠를 입고 갔다.

shirt.en: put a shirt on (very rare usage)

ex.en: He shirted himself quickly and rushed out the door.
ex.ko: 그는 재빨리 셔츠를 입고 문밖으로 뛰쳐나갔다.

shoulder.en: the part of the body between the neck and the arm

ex.en: She carried the bag on her shoulder.
She had to shoulder the blame for the mistake.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 실수에 대한 책임을 짊어져야 했다.

shrug.en: raise one's shoulders to indicate indifference or resignation

ex.en: She shrugged her shoulders.

shrug.en: a movement of raising your shoulders to show you don't know or don't care

ex.en: He answered with a shrug.

shut up.cue: 말하는 것을 멈추고 (구어체, 무례할 수 있음)
shut up.en: stop talking (often used as a rude command)

ex.en: Just shut up and listen for a second!
He tried to shut her up before she said too much.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀가 말을 너무 많이 하기 전에 입을 다물게 하려 했다.

shut up.en: (of a building) closed and not used for a long time

ex.en: The house has been shut up for years.
ex.ko: 그 집은 몇 년째 문을 닫은 채로 있다.

shy.en: nervous or uncomfortable around other people, especially people you don't know

ex.en: The little boy was too shy to say hello.
ex.ko: 그 어린 남자아이는 너무 수줍어서 인사를 못 했다.

shy.en: (of a horse) suddenly move back or aside in fright

ex.en: The horse shied at the loud noise.
ex.ko: 그 말은 큰 소리에 놀라 물러섰다.

sick.en: suffering from an illness or feeling unwell

ex.en: She stayed home because she was sick.

sick.en: annoyed or bored because you have had too much of something

ex.en: I'm sick of this rainy weather.
ex.ko: 나는 이 비 오는 날씨가 지긋지긋하다.

sick.en: people who are ill, as a group

ex.en: The hospital cares for the sick and injured.

side.en: a position to the left or right of something, or one surface of an object

ex.en: He stood on the other side of the room.

side.en: one of two or more groups that are opposed in a contest or argument

ex.en: Which side are you on?

side.en: located at or on the side of something

ex.en: Please use the side entrance.
Her sight is not as good as it used to be.

sight.en: something interesting or beautiful that you see, especially somewhere you are visiting

ex.en: The Grand Canyon is an amazing sight.

sight.en: see something, especially something you have been looking for

ex.en: The sailors sighted land after weeks at sea.
ex.ko: 선원들은 몇 주간의 항해 끝에 육지를 발견했다.

single.en: a hit in baseball that allows the batter to reach first base safely

ex.en: He hit a single to left field.
She has been single for two years.

single.en: only one; not more than one

ex.en: I didn't see a single mistake in his work.
ex.ko: 나는 그의 작업에서 실수를 단 하나도 보지 못했다.

single.en: (in baseball) hit the ball and safely reach first base

ex.en: He singled to right field in the ninth inning.

size.en: how big or small something is

ex.en: What size shoes do you wear?

size.en: (used in combination) having a specified size, such as 'king-size'

ex.en: The hotel room came with a king-size bed.
ex.ko: 그 호텔 방에는 킹사이즈 침대가 있었다.

skirt.en: a piece of clothing worn by women and girls that hangs from the waist

ex.en: She wore a blue skirt to school.
ex.ko: 그녀는 학교에 파란 치마를 입고 갔다.

skirt.cue: 민감한 문제를 정면으로 다루지 않고 피하다
skirt.en: avoid dealing with something directly, such as a difficult question

ex.en: The politician skirted the question about taxes.
ex.ko: 그 정치인은 세금에 관한 질문을 회피했다.

skylark.en: brown-speckled European lark noted for singing while hovering at a great height

ex.en: A skylark sang high above the field.

skylark.cue: (구어, 다소 옛스러운 표현) 신나게 장난치며 놀다
skylark.en: play in a noisy, cheerful way (informal, somewhat dated)

ex.en: The children skylarked around the playground.
The company announced a price slash.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 대폭적인 가격 삭감을 발표했어요.

slash.en: reduce something, such as a price or budget, by a large amount

ex.en: The store slashed prices by 50 percent.

slaughter.en: the killing of animals (as for food)

ex.en: The animals are raised for slaughter.

slaughter.cue: 고기를 얻기 위해 동물을 도살하다, 사람을 대량 학살하다
slaughter.en: kill animals for food, or kill a large number of people

ex.en: The cattle are slaughtered for meat.

slot.en: a narrow opening for putting something into, such as a coin or a letter

ex.en: Put a coin in the slot to start the machine.

slot.en: a particular time or place in a schedule or system

ex.en: I booked a slot for 3 p.m.

slot.en: arrange for something to happen at a particular time

ex.en: The meeting was slotted for 3 p.m.

small.en: not large in size or amount

ex.en: They live in a small apartment.

small.cue: 등에서 가장 잘록하게 들어간 부분 (the small of the back)
small.en: the narrowest part of the back, above the hips (mainly 'the small of the back')

ex.en: He felt a sharp pain in the small of his back.
ex.ko: 그는 등의 잘록한 부분에서 날카로운 통증을 느꼈다.

ex.en: She is a smart student.
He looked smart in his new suit.
ex.ko: 그는 새 정장을 입어 단정해 보였다.

ex.en: The cut on his hand still smarts.
ex.ko: 그의 손에 난 상처가 아직도 따끔거린다.

ex.en: He smashed the window.

smash.en: something, such as a film or song, that is extremely successful (informal, 'a smash')

ex.en: The movie was a smash at the box office.

smooth.en: having an even surface, without bumps or roughness

ex.en: The baby's skin was soft and smooth.
We had a smooth flight to Tokyo.

smooth.en: make something smooth, or remove difficulties from something

ex.en: She smoothed the wrinkles out of the tablecloth.

snake.en: a long, thin reptile with no legs that moves by sliding along the ground

ex.en: A snake crossed the path in front of us.
ex.ko: 뱀 한 마리가 우리 앞의 길을 가로질렀다.

snake.en: move or extend in a long, winding path

ex.en: The road snaked through the mountains.

snap.en: utter in an angry, sharp, or abrupt tone

ex.en: She snapped at him in frustration.

snap.ko: 아주 쉬운 일, 식은 죽 먹기(구어)
snap.en: something that is very easy to do (informal)

ex.en: Don't worry, the test will be a snap.
ex.ko: 걱정 마, 그 시험은 식은 죽 먹기일 거야.

sneer.en: a facial expression of contempt or scorn

ex.en: He gave a sneer of contempt.

sneer.en: smile or speak in a way that shows contempt

ex.en: He sneered at their attempt to help.

soaking.en: completely wet, often used before 'wet' for emphasis

ex.en: Come in, you're soaking wet!

soaking.en: the process of leaving something in liquid, or being made completely wet

ex.en: Give the beans a good soaking overnight.
ex.ko: 콩을 밤새 충분히 물에 담가 두어라.

social.en: relating to society, or relating to friendly activities with other people

ex.en: She has a busy social life.

social.en: an informal party or gathering held to bring people together

ex.en: The church is hosting a social on Friday evening.
ex.ko: 그 교회는 금요일 저녁에 친목 모임을 연다.

sock.en: a piece of clothing worn on the foot, inside a shoe

ex.en: He put on a pair of clean socks.
ex.ko: 그는 깨끗한 양말 한 켤레를 신었다.

ex.en: He socked the bully right in the nose.
ex.ko: 그는 그 불량배의 코를 세게 쳤다.

soft.en: not hard or firm; pleasant to touch

ex.en: The pillow is very soft.
She spoke in a soft voice.
He has a soft heart when it comes to animals.

soft.en: gently or quietly (informal, nonstandard variant of 'softly')

ex.en: Speak soft so you don't wake the baby.

solid.en: having a firm shape and structure, not liquid or gas

ex.en: Water becomes solid when it freezes.
They built a solid wooden table.

solid.en: reliable and based on good reasons or evidence

ex.en: The police have solid evidence against him.
ex.ko: 경찰은 그에 대한 확실한 증거를 가지고 있다.

solid.en: a three-dimensional shape, as studied in geometry

ex.en: A cube is a solid with six equal faces.
ex.ko: 정육면체는 여섯 개의 동일한 면을 가진 입체 도형이다.
